ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "abbreviation" TEXT /* Two-letter country abbreviation */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Full name of the country */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the geographic region */,
  "subregion_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the subregion within the geographic region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Total area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "government_type" TEXT /* Type of government (e.g., democracy, monarchy) */,
  "independence_date" TEXT /* Date of independence */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_subregion_id FOREIGN KEY ("subregion_id") REFERENCES subregions ("subregion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "happiness_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each happiness score entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the happiness score */,
  "happiness_score" REAL /* Overall happiness score */,
  "gdp_per_capita" REAL /* GDP per capita */,
  "social_support" REAL /* Social support index */,
  "life_expectancy" REAL /* Life expectancy index */,
  "freedom" REAL /* Freedom index */,
  "generosity" REAL /* Generosity index */,
  "corruption" REAL /* Corruption index */,
  "dystopia_residual" REAL /* Dystopia residual value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_happiness_scores_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subregions" (
  "subregion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subregion */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "subregion_name" TEXT /* Name of the subregion */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the subregion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subregion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subregions_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "currencies" (
  "currency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each currency */,
  "currency_name" TEXT /* Name of the currency */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the currency */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the currency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("currency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the language */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the language */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "governments" (
  "government_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each government type */,
  "government_type" TEXT /* Name of the government type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the government type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("government_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_currencies" (
  "country_currency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country-currency relationship */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "currency_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the currency */,
  "official_use" INTEGER /* Indicates if the currency is officially used in the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_currency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_currencies_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_currencies_currency_id FOREIGN KEY ("currency_id") REFERENCES currencies ("currency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_languages" (
  "country_language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country-language relationship */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the language */,
  "primary_language" INTEGER /* Indicates if the language is the primary language in the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_languages_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_languages_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("language_id") REFERENCES languages ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the user (e.g., university, organization) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "resource" TEXT /* Resource that the user has access to */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action that the user can perform on the resource */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "score_id" INTEGER /* ID of the happiness score being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the happiness score */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the happiness score was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_score_id FOREIGN KEY ("score_id") REFERENCES happiness_scores ("score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "score_sources" (
  "score_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each score-source relationship */,
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the happiness score */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_score_sources_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_score_sources_score_id FOREIGN KEY ("score_id") REFERENCES happiness_scores ("score_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "governance_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each governance indicator */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the governance indicator */,
  "corruption_perception" REAL /* Corruption perception index */,
  "press_freedom" REAL /* Press freedom index */,
  "civil_liberties" REAL /* Civil liberties index */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_governance_indicators_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)
